personas.name: Beauty-Brand KAM at L'Oréal-Tier MNC
personas.icp: Key Account Manager or Trade Marketing Lead at a tier-1 cosmetics/beauty MNC (L'Oréal, Shiseido, Coty) running SG cosmetics CPD/LUXE/PPD portfolios across Watsons + Guardian + department-store beauty counters; owns share-of-shelf, MSL compliance, and NPI counter-launches across 60–200 outlets.
pains: Counter staff attrition during CNY/Christmas peaks costs 2–4 missed outlet-weeks per peak — each worth ~SGD 18k revenue impact per L'Oréal post-mortem benchmarks
RFP scorecards now demand mobile + GPS + planogram + BI dashboard for HQ; legacy agencies with Excel + WhatsApp fail procurement gate before pricing review
Counter conversion sensitive to language fluency (Mandarin + Bahasa); generic SG promoter rosters under-deliver in Chinese-majority and Malay-majority neighbourhoods
Monthly retailer-side share-of-shelf scorecards from Watsons/Guardian now arrive; brand HQ expects agency cadence to match — weekly Excel decks are too slow
Procurement consolidation under L'Oréal SEA roster review demands fewer-larger agencies with multi-category coverage across CPD + LUXE + PPD + ACD
personas.current_workaround: Mix of in-house counter promoters on L'Oréal HRIS (CPD only) + Smollan-CDM regional MSA (LUXE, PPD) routed via SG team + occasional gig-platform freelancers for activation peaks. Reporting via mixed Excel + Smollan FieldX deck + monthly steering call.

nba.summary: Direct displacement of Smollan-CDM regional MSA bench in SG; Smollan billed at ~SGD 5,500/pax/mo loaded.

personas.name: Trade-Marketing Lead at Pernod Ricard-Tier Liquor Co.
personas.icp: Trade Marketing Manager at a multinational wines/spirits company (Pernod Ricard, Diageo, Suntory) managing SG modern-trade + on-trade execution; runs sustained shelf presence + promo compliance + NPI launches across FairPrice + Cold Storage + Giant + selective on-trade activation.
pains: Promo-compliance windows in supermarkets are narrow (3–14 days); missed window = lost promo budget reconciliation with HQ Finance, ~SGD 30k+ per major SKU
Liquor-category regulations + age-restricted sampling demand trained promoters — generic gig-platform freelancers don't pass internal compliance
BI dashboard demand from regional HQ (Hong Kong / Paris) — they want PowerBI feed not PDF, on weekly cadence
PWM wage inflation on in-house counter team forecast +5–6%/yr through 2027 → TCO of in-house model rising faster than agency rate cards
Multi-country brand wants SEA expansion roadmap but SG-only agencies cannot extend into MY/TH/VN without entity setup
personas.current_workaround: Single SG agency (currently mid-tier — RSG or Engage class) on dedicated 15–25 pax bench, weekly Excel reports + photo upload via WhatsApp, monthly steering deck. Reports manually re-keyed into PowerBI by category manager.

nba.summary: Existing mid-tier SG agency bench + brand-side category-manager re-keying time + separate PowerBI consultant invoice.

tooling_lines.tool: Current agency dedicated bench, 20 pax × SGD 4,200/mo
External PowerBI consultant retainer for dashboard maintenance

personas.name: House-Brand Mgr at FairPrice / Retail House-Brand
personas.icp: House-brand category manager at a SG retailer (FairPrice Group, DFI Cold Storage/Giant) running house-brand merchandising across own-store network; conflict-of-interest constraints prevent using merchandisers shared with competing brands.
pains: House-brand promoters need to be sequestered from competitor brand promoters — cannot pool with agency bench serving Unilever/P&G
Internal headcount allocation under NTUC Enterprise / DFI cost-control limits hiring; outsourcing under tight conflict-of-interest guardrails
Cross-functional accountability — house-brand performance measured against external brands in same category in same store
Tech stack needs to integrate with retailer-side data (loyalty card SKU velocity) rather than just shelf-share scorecards
personas.current_workaround: Mix of FairPrice-internal merchandisers (priority house-brand placement) + a sequestered agency contract on rotational basis (currently SGD 4,000/pax loaded equivalent).

nba.summary: Marginal headcount via FairPrice retail-operations or sequestered agency contract; alternative is loaded-cost internal hire.

personas.name: Indie / Mid-Market FMCG Founder (Oatside-Tier)
personas.icp: Founder or growth-stage GM at a Series A–C SG-incorporated indie FMCG brand (Oatside-class oat-milk, indie beauty, F&B challenger) entering or expanding modern-trade presence with <5 pax field execution need across selective outlets.
pains: Dedicated-team minimums (15–20 pax) at tier-1 agencies are 5x over actual outlet coverage need — pricing wall blocks entry
Founder-time tax — running gig-platform freelancers + supervisor-by-text consumes 6–10 hr/week of founder/marketing-lead time
Investor-board pressure to show modern-trade growth → execution quality must hold up but budget runway constraints are real
PSG / EDG eligibility relevant — net price after grant materially shifts the decision
Cycle-time + outlet-level photo audit needs to plug into the brand's existing analytics stack (Notion / Looker)
personas.current_workaround: Gig-platform promoters (FastJobs/Workclass) at SGD 12–18/hr + part-time supervisor on retainer + founder-led WhatsApp coordination + monthly Google Sheet audit. Effective load ~SGD 3,500–7,000/mo across 1–3 outlet groups.

nba.summary: Gig-platform freelancers + part-time supervisor + founder coordination tax + manual reporting.

tooling_lines.tool: Lost-outlet-week risk (1 missed week / quarter × SGD 6k impact / 3 months)

personas.name: Regional SEA Trade-Marketing Director
personas.icp: Regional trade-marketing/commercial director at a tier-1 FMCG MNC (Unilever, Reckitt, Coty, Pernod Ricard) based in SG, responsible for SG + at least 1–2 additional SEA markets (MY + TH most common); owns the bundling decision on whether SG agency extends across SEA execution.
pains: Vendor consolidation mandate from HQ — each additional SEA-country vendor doubles compliance overhead and steering meetings
Cross-country reporting consistency: SG agency reports in PowerBI, MY agency in Excel, TH in PDF → manual stitching by regional analyst
PWM SG + similar wage-floor regulations across MY/ID/TH creating cost-base divergence; need vendor that can present unified TCO across markets
SEA expansion plan demands an agency with footprint or partner relationship beyond SG — pure-SG agency forces them to onboard a second vendor
Procurement / Finance demands consolidated invoicing across markets — multi-vendor manual reconciliation is broken
personas.current_workaround: Smollan-CDM regional MSA covering 3–6 markets bundled with global agreement; or stitched-together SG + MY + TH country agencies coordinated by regional analyst. Reporting harmonised manually by regional team in Tableau.

nba.summary: Smollan-CDM or DKSH regional MSA bundle; ~SGD 5,500–5,800/pax/mo across the bundle with global overhead.

pricing_models.name: Per-pax dedicated monthly
pricing_models.rationale: Standard FMCG agency model — bills per dedicated merchandiser headcount per month, includes supervision + reporting + EMR-Intel. Maps directly to brand's planogram + outlet-coverage planning. Default unit for procurement RFP scoring.

pricing_models.name: Shared bench monthly retainer
pricing_models.rationale: Up-to-6-pax shared promoter bench priced as monthly retainer (not per-pax) — the only mid-market model that fits Oatside-class brands needing 1–3 outlet groups without paying for unused bench. EMR-Intel reporting bundled.

pricing_models.name: Project-based (NPI launch / POSM / audit)
pricing_models.rationale: Discrete project pricing for NPI launches, POSM rollouts, and merchandising audits — sized to scope not headcount. Useful as add-on to dedicated/shared base, less useful as standalone retainer.

pricing_models.name: EMR-Intel platform license (standalone)
pricing_models.rationale: Standalone field-force SaaS license for brand or another agency to run their own bench through EMR-Intel. Higher-margin platform revenue but fights Trax/ShelfPerfect/Repsly head-on; best positioned as Y3 group-internal license or selective platform-only deal.

pricing_models.name: Regional SEA bundle (per-pax × markets)
pricing_models.rationale: Multi-country dedicated bundle priced per-pax-per-market with consolidated invoicing and unified BI; leverages Elitez Group cross-border footprint. The structural answer to Smollan-CDM and DKSH global MSAs.

elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Tier-1 beauty MNC budgets locked through annual planning cycle; the SGD 1,000/pax delta between SG-local agencies (SGD 4,200) and Smollan-CDM (SGD 5,500) has not moved L'Oréal/Shiseido bench placements between 2022–25 per buyer interviews. Decision-driver is procurement-gate (tech stack + retention metric + ESG), not price. Once gate passed, ~±15% price tolerance.

elasticity_heuristics.segment: Trade-Marketing Lead at Pernod Ricard-Tier Liquor Co.
elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Liquor-category trade-marketing budgets visible quarterly; pricing-page reaction sensitivity confirmed by 3 buyer interviews 2026-Q1 — willing to switch agency on SGD 400/pax delta but only if tech parity. PWM rolling pressure on in-house cost means agency rate-cards are evaluated against rising in-house baseline.

elasticity_heuristics.segment: House-Brand Mgr at FairPrice / Retail House-Brand
elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Retailer cost-control culture (NTUC Enterprise + DFI both publicly cost-disciplined per FY24 ARs). Buyer sensitivity moderate; willingness-to-pay highly dependent on conflict-of-interest segregation guarantee.

Founder discretionary spend on field execution typically SGD 4,000–8,000/mo ceiling; PSG-funded effective price (50% net) is a step-function reversal of WTP. SG indie F&B founder Slack threads Q1 2026 repeatedly cite price as primary objection to agency engagement. 9 of 35 Elitez inbound mid-market leads Q4 2025 cited 'cost over headcount minimum' as discovery-call objection.

Regional bundles billed against centralised SEA-region OPEX line; per-pax pricing within ±10% of Smollan-CDM benchmark is acceptable for vendor-consolidation rationale. Decision driver is multi-market coordination + reporting consistency, not pax rate. ~SGD 700/pax delta against Smollan absorbed for bundle benefits in two reference buyer interviews 2026-Q1.

recommended_tiers.name: Shared 6-Pax Bench
what_in: Up-to-6 pax shared promoter bench across supermarket + pharmacy + convenience
Trained supervisor and routing coordination
EMR-Intel mobile app (EN/CN/BM) + attendance + photo + planogram
QuickView back-office for buyer brand manager
Monthly BI summary (PowerBI/Tableau-compatible)
Same-week deployment
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Cheaper than hiring a full-time merchandiser; covers 1–3 outlet groups without the dedicated-team minimum.

recommended_tiers.name: Dedicated 5–60 Pax
what_in: Per-pax/mo loaded billing for dedicated bench (5–60 pax)
Trained, multilingual (EN/CN/BM) merchandisers/promoters
Free replacement of under-performing pax
>95% retention SLA
EMR-Intel: mobile + QuickView + BI dashboards (executive, weekly call achievement, MSL, share-of-shelf, OOS, promo compliance)
Supervisor + routing + training + WSG/SkillsFuture funded upskilling
Procurement gate: ISO 9001, ISO 27001, Cyber Essentials, EcoVadis Bronze
what_excluded: Cross-border SEA execution (see Regional SEA Bundle)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: ~SGD 1,000/pax below Smollan-CDM MSA rate-card, with SG-pure bench depth + retention SLA + EMR-Intel parity.

recommended_tiers.name: Enterprise Trade Marketing
recommended_tiers.target_persona: Trade-Marketing Lead at Pernod Ricard-Tier Liquor Co.
what_in: Per-pax/mo dedicated bench (15–40 pax typical)
Category-trained promoters (liquor / beauty / personal-care / household specialisation)
All Dedicated 5–60 Pax contents
Custom KPI rubric design quarterly
Daily live BI dashboard refresh
Named senior account director + weekly steering call
POSM + NPI launch coverage included in scope
Free promoter compliance refreshers (liquor / age-restricted SKUs / pharma OTC)
what_excluded: EMR-Intel standalone license to third party
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: 1 missed outlet-week avoided (~SGD 18k) pays the annual uplift over a mid-tier SG agency.

recommended_tiers.name: Regional SEA Bundle
what_in: Per-pax/mo (SG benchmark; MY/TH/VN/PH priced to local cost base)
Consolidated invoicing across markets via Elitez Group entities
Unified EMR-Intel BI dashboard across markets
Single SG-based named account director
Cross-border supervisor coordination
Pan-SEA KPI scorecard at brand HQ
Quarterly governance review
what_excluded: Local-language counter staff (handled at local-market level)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Replaces Smollan-CDM / DKSH global MSA at SG-decision quality without the regional MSA overhead.

grants.name: PSG (Productivity Solutions Grant)
grants.eligibility: SG-registered SME, IMDA-pre-approved digital solution. EMR-Intel submission to IMDA PSG pre-approval is the Y1 critical path; not currently on list (2026-05).

grants.name: EDG (Enterprise Development Grant)
grants.eligibility: SG-registered SME; project-based under 'Strategic Brand & Marketing Development' or 'Productivity' pillars. Customer-led submission.

grants.name: WSG WDG(JR+) — Job Redesign+ pre-approved consultant track
grants.eligibility: Funded when merchandising engagement is part of a broader workforce/role-redesign project. Delivered under Elitez Group's pre-approved consultant LOA (valid 18 Mar 2026 – 17 Mar 2027).

grants.name: SkillsFuture Workforce Training Grant (operational, not customer-facing)
grants.eligibility: Funds Elitez bench training. Reduces COGS not customer price; underpins the SGD 4,500/pax rate floor.
